items.title: Pin core deps
items.prefer: Exact pins (==) for what you've tested, ranges only where intentional.
items.example:
```
openai==1.40.0
httpx==0.27.2
numpy>=1.24,<2     # range only because we test against both
```

items.title: Curate vs freeze
items.prefer: Maintain direct deps by hand; generate the full pinned set with a tool.
items.why: Keeps intent clear and transitive noise out of your edits.
items.example: `requirements.in` (your hand-edited list of *direct* deps) → `pip-compile requirements.in` → `requirements.txt` (auto-pinned full closure).

items.title: Layer environments
items.avoid: Mixing dev tools into production requirements.
items.example:
```
requirements.txt        # httpx, openai
dev-requirements.txt    # -r requirements.txt + pytest, mypy, ruff
```
Prod installs only the first; dev installs the second.

items.title: Always isolate
items.prefer: pip inside an activated virtual environment.
items.why: Avoids conflicts and protects the OS Python.
items.example: Every project starts with `python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate` *before* `pip install`. Habit, not afterthought.
